html:
Nitre is a Mineral resource obtained by mining smooth Boulders, which will drop at least one (along with other minerals) upon being mined. It also occasionally falls during underground Earthquakes. It can be used to craft Salt Licks and Gunpowder.

Downloadable Content

In the Reign of Giants DLC, it can be used to make Endothermic Fires, Endothermic Fire Pits and the Morning Star. Endothermic Fires can also be refueled with Nitre. Nitre can also be obtained by digging up a Moleworm's Burrow. It can be used as a fuel for Ice Flingomatics to refuel 19%.

In the Shipwrecked DLC, it can also be used to make Endothermic Fires and Endothermic Fire Pits, and Nitre can additionally be obtained by digging a Magma Pile. Gold-flecked Magma Piles will drop Gold Nuggets instead of Nitre. Unlike Boulders, magma piles do not have a preset number of Nitre to drop, instead, each item dropped has an independent chance to be Nitre. Therefore, there is a small chance get no Nitre, and a very small chance to get 9 Nitre from a Magma Pile.

In the Hamlet DLC, Nitre is required to craft Lawn Turf and several variations of Hedges. Additionally, Wormwood uses Nitre to craft Compost Wraps.

Trivia

  • Nitre was added in the A Little Rain Must Fall update.
  • Nitre was changed to be dropped by the small, smooth boulder in The End of the Beginning update.
  • Nitre, a.k.a. Saltpeter (Potassium Nitrate, KNO₃), is a common ingredient in creating gunpowder in the real world. It is also an ingredient in fertilizer. Although it cannot be directly used as fertilizer in game, it can be used to craft Compost Wrap, which can be used as fertilizer in Don't Starve Together.

Nitre is a Mineral resource obtained by mining smooth Boulders, which will drop at least one (along with other minerals) upon being mined. It also occasionally falls during underground Earthquakes. It can be used to craft Salt Licks and Gunpowder.
Downloadable Content[edit | edit source]
In the Reign of Giants DLC, it can be used to make Endothermic Fires, Endothermic Fire Pits and the Morning Star. Endothermic Fires can also be refueled with Nitre. Nitre can also be obtained by digging up a Moleworm's Burrow. It can be used as a fuel for Ice Flingomatics to refuel 19%.
In the Shipwrecked DLC, it can also be used to make Endothermic Fires and Endothermic Fire Pits, and Nitre can additionally be obtained by digging a Magma Pile. Gold-flecked Magma Piles will drop Gold Nuggets instead of Nitre. Unlike Boulders, magma piles do not have a preset number of Nitre to drop, instead, each item dropped has an independent chance to be Nitre. Therefore, there is a small chance get no Nitre, and a very small chance to get 9 Nitre from a Magma Pile.
In the Hamlet DLC, Nitre is required to craft Lawn Turf and several variations of Hedges. Additionally, Wormwood uses Nitre to craft Compost Wraps.

Trivia[edit | edit source]
Nitre was added in the A Little Rain Must Fall update.
Nitre was changed to be dropped by the small, smooth boulder in The End of the Beginning update.
Nitre, a.k.a. Saltpeter (Potassium Nitrate, KNO₃), is a common ingredient in creating gunpowder in the real world. It is also an ingredient in fertilizer. Although it cannot be directly used as fertilizer in game, it can be used to craft Compost Wrap, which can be used as fertilizer in Don't Starve Together.

Heh... |wanda = A rock of some sort. |choose = wolfgang}} '''Nitre''' is a [[:Category:Minerals|Mineral]] resource obtained by mining smooth [[Boulders]], which will drop at least one (along with other minerals) upon being mined. It also occasionally falls during [[Caves|underground]] [[Earthquake]]s. It can be used to craft [[Salt Lick]]s and [[Gunpowder]]. __NOTOC__ =={{pic32|Gift Icon}} Downloadable Content== In the ''[[Don't Starve: Reign of Giants|Reign of Giants]]'' DLC, it can be used to make [[Endothermic Fire]]s, [[Endothermic Fire#Endothermic Fire Pit|Endothermic Fire Pits]] and the [[Morning Star]]. Endothermic Fires can also be refueled with Nitre. Nitre can also be obtained by [[Shovel|digging]] up a [[Moleworm]]'s [[Burrow]]. It can be used as a fuel for [[Ice Flingomatic|Ice Flingomatics]] to refuel 19%. In the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLC, it can also be used to make [[Endothermic Fire]]s and [[Endothermic Fire#Endothermic Fire Pit|Endothermic Fire Pits]], and Nitre can additionally be obtained by digging a [[Magma Pile]]. Gold-flecked Magma Piles will drop [[Gold Nugget]]s instead of Nitre. Unlike Boulders, magma piles do not have a preset number of Nitre to drop, instead, each item dropped has an independent chance to be Nitre. Therefore, there is a small chance get no Nitre, and a very small chance to get 9 Nitre from a Magma Pile. In the ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet|Hamlet]]'' DLC, Nitre is required to craft [[Lawn Turf]] and several variations of [[Hedge]]s. Additionally, [[Wormwood]] uses Nitre to craft [[Compost Wrap]]s. =={{pic32|Icon Tools}} Usage== {{Recipe |item1 = Nitre |count1 = 4 |item2 = Boards |count2 = 2 |tool = Alchemy Engine |result = Salt Lick}} {{Recipe |item1 = Nitre |item2 = Charcoal |item3 = Rotten Egg |tool = Alchemy Engine |result = Gunpowder}} {{Recipe |item1 = Nitre |count1 = 2 |item2 = Cut Grass |count2 = 3 |tool = Science Machine |result = Endothermic Fire |dlc = Reign of Giants}} {{Recipe |item1 = Nitre |count1 = 2 |item2 = Cut Stone |count2 = 4 |item3 = Electrical Doodad |count3 = 2 |tool = Alchemy Engine |result = Endothermic Fire Pit |dlc = Reign of Giants}} {{Recipe |item1 = Nitre |count1 = 2 |item2 = Electrical Doodad |count2 = 2 |item3 = Volt Goat Horn |tool = Alchemy Engine |result = Morning Star |dlc = Reign of Giants}} {{Recipe |item1 = Nitre |count1 = 1 |item2 = Cut Grass |count2 = 2 |tool = Alchemy Engine |result = Lawn Turf |dlc = Hamlet}} {{Recipe |item1 = Nitre |item2 = Clippings |count2 = 9 |tool = Key to the City |result = Block Hedge |resultcount = 3 |dlc = Hamlet}} {{Recipe |item1 = Nitre |item2 = Clippings |count2 = 9 |tool = Key to the City |result = Cone Hedge |resultcount = 3 |dlc = Hamlet}} {{Recipe |item1 = Nitre |item2 = Clippings |count2 = 9 |tool = Key to the City |result = Layered Hedge |resultcount = 3 |dlc = Hamlet}} {{Recipe |item1 = Nitre |item2 = Rot |count2 = 2 |item3 = Manure |count3 = 5 |result = Compost Wrap |dlc = Hamlet |character = Wormwood}} =={{pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia== * Nitre was added in the ''[[Version History#March 12, 2013 - A Little Rain Must Fall|A Little Rain Must Fall]]'' update. * Nitre was changed to be dropped by the small, smooth boulder in ''[[Version History#April 20, 2013 - The End of the Beginning.5B3.5D|The End of the Beginning]]'' update. * Nitre, a.k.a. Saltpeter (Potassium Nitrate, KNO₃), is a common ingredient in creating gunpowder in the real world. It is also an ingredient in fertilizer. Although it cannot be directly used as fertilizer in game, it can be used to craft [[Compost Wrap]], which can be used as fertilizer in ''[[Don't Starve Together]]''.
